singleStoreVerdict.caller: sling / order-dispatch (InstantiateSlingFormula, dispatchWisp, doStartGraphWorkflow, graph-v2 root helpers)
singleStoreVerdict.evidence: Every graph write in this surface acts on a STATICALLY-KNOWN graph artifact, so a separate graphStore param suffices — no by-id routing object. sling.go:1287 molecule.Instantiate: the caller already computes graphWorkflow:=graphroute.IsCompiledGraphWorkflow(recipe) at sling.go:1247 BEFORE the write, so it can pick deps.GraphStore vs deps.Store. order_dispatch.go:1410 dispatchWisp ALREADY takes workStore as a param distinct from orderStore (existence proof of the param-threading shape); the wisp pour is class-homogeneous graph. order_dispatch.go:1435 workStore.Update(rootID) hits the gcg- root just minted in Instantiate. sling_core.go:631 doStartGraphWorkflow is gated on mResult.GraphWorkflow so all its store ops target the graph root. The mixed sites (sling_core.go:645 SetMetadata(sourceBeadID,work) inside doStartGraphWorkflow; sling_core.go:830 withSourceWorkflowLaunchLock work-source-read + graph-root-list) are each class-decidable at the call site -> two params, no federation. CAVEAT verified: order path is safe (targetID=='' skips NormalizeInputConvoy), but graphv2.PrepareInvocation/NormalizeInputConvoy with targetID!='' does a work-target read AND a graph synthetic-convoy write in one store param — that helper must gain a 2nd store param or be split (internal/graphv2/invocation.go), pushing this past 5 files unless invocation.go is a separate phase.

singleStoreVerdict.caller: dispatch.ProcessControl — graph-pure handlers (retry, ralph, check, retry-eval, fanout, tally, scope-check, workflow-finalize)
singleStoreVerdict.evidence: Every control bead carries gc.root_bead_id => it and its whole subtree (subjects/attempts/evals/scope bodies/voters/sinks) classify ClassGraph (coordclass classify arms verified at classify.go:118 isWorkflowMetadata, :125 typeConvergence). So threading a single graphStore makes these handlers class-aware trivially: control.go:514 molecule.Attach, retry.go:521/527 store.Create, fanout.go:174 InstantiateFragment, ralph.go:610 ApplyGraphPlan all just need the GRAPH store as create/apply target (the graph SQLite/PG store is itself graph-apply-capable via beads.GraphApplyFor, replacing routedGraphApplier with zero routing logic). runtime.go:441 liveListForRoot picks graph-vs-work by rootID prefix (G2c). workflow-finalize (runtime.go:747/989) already crosses stores via opts.ResolveStoreRef/SourceWorkflowStores and never depended on single-store class routing — unaffected.

VERIFIED genuinely cross-class within a SINGLE store call. internal/convoy/membership.go:68 Members does store.List(ParentID=convoyID) + store.DepList(convoyID,down) [graph convoy] then loops dep.DependsOnID and store.Get(memberID) at membership.go:102 [WORK backlog member] — graph and work in one call. membership.go:24 TrackItem: store.Get(itemID)[work] + store.DepAdd(convoyID,itemID)[cross-class edge graph->work]. drain.go interleaves store.Get(rootID/row.UnitConvoyID/row.ItemRootID) (gcg- graph, lines 67/121/338/409/477) with store.Get(row.MemberID) (work, lines 269/1107/1145) in the SAME handler. drain.go:1121 reserveDrainMembers SetMetadata(member.ID) must hit WORK; drain.go:855 ensureDrainUnitConvoy Create must hit GRAPH. No single param-class is correct for these; they need per-id class dispatch (a 2-element [graph,work] probe / prefix-route).

VERIFIED single-store, class-mixed by construction. molecule.go:663 store.Create(b) in the recipe sequential fallback services graph.v2 steps, convergence wisps (opts.DeferAssignees), AND legacy work recipes through the SAME line — per-bead class only knowable via coordclass.Classify(b). Confirmed reachable on the legacy/work path (formula_v2=false, GraphApplyFor false, or DeferAssignees=true). molecule.go:1296 markFailed(store, createdIDs) inherits this: the ids can be work OR graph depending on the path. molecule.Instantiate has exactly ONE store param (verified call sites: sling.go:1287, order_dispatch.go:1410, drain.go:940, cmd_order.go:705, cmd_formula.go:711) — making it class-aware requires either inlining Classify+per-bead store-pick (= the Router's Create logic) or handing it BOTH stores + classify, churning the signature across all callers.

VERIFIED arbitrary-root, cross-class. Non-test callers: sling_attachment.go:154 CloseSubtree(store, attached.ID) via CloseAttachedSubtree (called from wisp_autoclose.go:180 and sling_attachment.go:206/278), and molecule_autoclose.go:203 ListSubtree. cleanup.go:29 store.Get(rootID) + cleanup.go:58 store.Children(WithBothTiers) + cleanup.go:144 store.CloseAll(ordered cross-class ids). CloseAll over ids spanning work+graph is the killer: a single graph store closes only its own ids and silently leaves work members open (or vice-versa) — exactly the cleanup-incompleteness bug. Router.CloseAll groups by backendForID; a single param cannot reproduce it. The store handed here IS routedPolicyStore-wrapped today (verified main.go:1248), so it federates via the Router now; deleting the Router strands the other class.

ownerDecision:
OWNER DECISION REQUIRED — three single-store call sites (molecule.Instantiate recipe sequential-fallback, molecule CloseSubtree/ListSubtree, dispatch drain via convoy.Members/TrackItem) CANNOT be made class-aware by separate work/graph params alone. Each does per-id, class-mixed store access in one call (Create of a per-bead-unknown-class bead; CloseAll over ids spanning both classes; graph-convoy DepList + work-member Get). Making them param-class-aware = inlining coordclass.Classify + a 2-element store probe = re-implementing the Router's by-id logic inside Layer-2 packages.

OPTIONS:
(A) Build a FOCUSED 2-backend routing store (the rejected Path-B graphRoutedStore{work,graph}). PRO: localizes ALL by-id/CloseAll/Members ambiguity behind one tiny object; molecule/dispatch/sling signatures stay beads.Store; byte-identical at graph=bd (returns work). It is NOT the full coordrouter — no federated List/Ready, no Create-by-class fan, just by-id Get/DepList/CloseAll prefix-route over [graph,work] + a graph-targeted Create/ApplyGraphPlan. CON: it is literally the design the owner rejected; it is a routing-store object built silently if not surfaced.
(B) Keep coordrouter for GRAPH ONLY; do NOT delete it. PRO: zero churn to molecule/dispatch/sling/drain; the verified-correct Router.CloseAll/backendForID/Members federation stays; lowest blast radius on a live 6-rig city. CON: the migration's stated goal (delete coordrouter) is not met; the Router survives as the graph-class router.
(C) Churn molecule/dispatch/sling signatures to thread BOTH stores + push coordclass.Classify into Layer-2 (molecule.go, cleanup.go, drain.go, convoy/membership.go + all callers). PRO: no routing object; explicit class-awareness. CON: re-implements Router logic in 4+ packages; convoy.Members/TrackItem are shared primitives — changing them ripples beyond dispatch; blows the 5-file/phase budget; HIGHEST risk of a misroute orphaning live legacy SQLite data; markFailed/CloseAll per-id classification is exactly the judgment-in-Go the project forbids.

RECOMMENDATION: Option A, reframed honestly to the owner as the only faithful, low-risk replacement for the three irreducibly by-id sites — NOT silently built. The owner's rule ('STOP and report, do not silently build a routing-store') is satisfied by surfacing it. The graph-pure dispatch handlers and the entire sling/order surface go class-aware with separate params (no object) per the verdicts above; the routing store is needed ONLY for drain's convoy.Members/TrackItem, molecule's recipe-fallback Create+markFailed, and CloseSubtree/CloseAll. Scope it to by-id Get/DepList/DepAdd/CloseAll + graph-Create/ApplyGraphPlan over exactly [work,graph], no List/Ready federation (those are handled by G2c's GraphOnlyListFor forwarder). If the owner holds the no-object line, fall back to Option B (keep coordrouter for graph) — Option C is not advisable for an irreversible live migration.
g2aPlan:
G2a — stop graph CREATE/apply orphaning onto WORK after the Router is gone. ROOT CAUSE (verified bead_policy_store.go:68-71): beadPolicyStore.Create calls createWithStoragePolicy(s.Store, b, storage) unconditionally; beadPolicyGraphStore.ApplyGraphPlan (bead_policy_store.go:243) delegates destination entirely to s.applier sourced from beads.GraphApplyFor(store) at wrap time (bead_policy_store.go:49). Today store=Router so graph routes correctly; after Router deletion store=work => graph creates/applies orphan onto the Dolt work store.

FIX (mirror the existing class-aware seam, no new abstraction):
1. bead_policy_store.go: add fields `cityPath string` and `graphStore beads.Store` to beadPolicyStore (struct at :28). Change signature to wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies(store, cfg, cityPath). Inside: resolve graphStore := resolveGraphStore(store, cfg, cityPath, nil) ONCE (class_store.go:282 — returns store itself when !graphRelocated, so byte-identical at graph=bd). Source the applier from beads.GraphApplyFor(graphStore) not GraphApplyFor(store).
2. beadPolicyStore.Create: classify via coordclass.Classify(b); if ==coordclass.ClassGraph route createWithStoragePolicy(s.graphStore, b, storage) else s.Store. (classify arms verified classify.go:88-133: wisp/workflow/synthetic-convoy/convergence => ClassGraph.)
3. Thread cityPath through the 3 prod call files: api_state.go (sites :221[deleted in G3b], :401 use cs.cityPath, :477/:484/:513 use cs.cityPath; routedPolicyStore body :253/:257 already has cityPath param), cmd_convoy_dispatch.go (controlStoreWithGraphRouting already has cityPath). main.go needs NO edit (routes via routedPolicyStore unchanged signature).
PHASING: G2a-1 = bead_policy_store.go + api_state.go + cmd_convoy_dispatch.go (3 prod files). G2a-2 = test arity fixes (bead_policy_store_test.go, bead_policy_store_conformance_test.go, wisp_autoclose_test.go, api_state_router_test.go, api_state_test.go = exactly 5 test files, at cap).
ADVERSARIAL RISK: (i) DOUBLE-ROUTING if G2a lands while Router still exists (Router routes graph, then policy re-routes) — both point at the same legacy SQLite so likely idempotent, but MUST land G2a together with G3a or gate on store-not-a-Router. (ii) policyForCreate wisp-root special-case does s.Get(rootID) on the embedded WORK store — if wisp roots are graph-resident this misses; verify (the Router never routed Get in Create either, so likely pre-existing, but confirm). (iii) CLASSIFICATION-DRIFT: Classify's ClassGraph is broader than policyNameForBead (adds synthetic convoys + convergence) — these now route to graph; matches today's Router behavior for opted-in cities, so not new, but confirm effectiveBeadStorage returns a sane tier for them. (iv) LEGACY-LOCATION landmine: resolveGraphStore calls openGraphSQLiteStore directly (legacy .gc/beads.sqlite); any refactor to resolveClassStore silently orphans live graph=sqlite data.
g3Plan:
G3 — delete internal/coordrouter (irreversible, ships live to 6-rig maintainer-city). GATING: deletion is the FINAL step, gated on G2a (CREATE/apply chokepoint moved to class-aware policy Create), G2b (by-id [graph,work] in beadStoresForID), G2c (ListGraphOnly forwarder), AND the routing-store/Option-A-or-B owner decision for the 3 irreducibly-by-id sites (drain/convoy.Members, molecule recipe-fallback Create+markFailed, CloseSubtree/CloseAll). Do NOT delete coordrouter until those by-id sites have a verified-correct cross-class path (routing store OR Option-B keep-graph-router).

G3a (Router removal from production wiring, api_state.go — the ONE prod blocker, verified): routedPolicyStore (:251) collapses to `return wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies(workBackend, cfg, cityPath)` (the policy wrapper now owns graph routing); delete registerGraphStoreBackend/registerGraphStoreSQLite (:336/:360). wrapWithCachingStore (:166-234): delete the existingRouter swap (:198-202) and the in-place Register in finish() (:219-221) — finish collapses to `return routedPolicyStore(cs, policyStore.cfg, cityPath)` for both arms (graph stays uncached automatically because it's re-resolved via resolveGraphStore, returning the shared graphStoreHandleCache handle, never a CachingStore). closeBeadStoreHandle (:803-831): delete the *coordrouter.Router arm (:810-821); the unwrapBeadPolicyStore + CachingStore arms + noCloseSQLiteStore no-op cover the rest (graph handle is shared/process-owned, correctly NOT closed per-store). Remove the now-dangling coordrouter import.

G3b (the surrounding packages): coordclass SURVIVES (storemigrate imports it, no coordrouter). ClassifyGraphPlan becomes Router-exclusive-dead UNLESS the G2a graph-apply chokepoint calls coordclass.ClassifyGraphPlan to pick the graph store — preserve the plan-class decision there. storeref SURVIVES; delete storeref_test.go:TestResolve_MatchesRouterGet (self-deleting differential) + its coordrouter import; keep TestPrefixOwner/TestResolve_FederationFallback. coordtest/conformance.go:175/181 — retarget func() coordrouter.GraphStore -> func() beads.GraphApplyStore (structurally identical) and drop the import. codectest survives untouched (comment-only ref). Rewrite/delete the test importers: api_state_router_test.go (21 refs — wiring contract, some asserts INVERT), session_router_test.go, bead_http_graph_store_test.go, cmd_close_test.go, cmd_ready_test.go, graph_store_resolver_test.go, dispatch integration tests, molecule/instantiate_router_integration_test.go, beads conformance tests. FINAL: rm -rf internal/coordrouter/.
ADVERSARIAL RISK: Create-by-class + by-id-mutation + Claimer/EnvActorClaimer bridge currently have NO non-Router differential — write the replacement (against the post-G2 store wiring / the routing store from the owner decision) BEFORE deleting router_*_test.go or coverage is lost. The graph-only-list capability MUST survive on the post-G3 wrapped store (see G2c load-bearing risk).
conformanceTest:
RELOCATED-GRAPH CONFORMANCE TEST (the safety net byte-identity-at-bd CANNOT provide — at graph=bd the graph store IS the work store so misclassification is invisible). It MUST run with [beads] graph_store="sqlite" against a REAL 2-store setup (Dolt/Mem work + SQLite graph at the LEGACY <cityPath>/.gc/beads.sqlite), and assert PHYSICAL store residence, not just logical visibility:
1. CREATE routing: pour a graph.v2/wisp molecule via molecule.Instantiate AND a legacy work recipe through the SAME entrypoint; assert every gcg-/workflow/wisp/synthetic-convoy/convergence bead physically lands in the SQLite file and every work-class bead in the Dolt store. Specifically exercise the recipe SEQUENTIAL FALLBACK (force formula_v2=false / GraphApplyFor=false / DeferAssignees=true) so molecule.go:663 is hit — assert no gcg- bead leaks into the work store (the orphan landmine).
2. ApplyGraphPlan: assert the atomic graph pour lands wholly in SQLite (ClassifyGraphPlan decision preserved); a plan with embedded work-typed steps stays intra-SQLite.
3. by-id mutate: Update/Close/Reopen/Assign/Claim/ReleaseIfCurrent/SetMetadata on a gcg- id resolves to SQLite (G2b [graph,work] order) without forking the work `bd` store; a stray gcg- id present only in work still resolves (federation fallback).
4. CloseSubtree/CloseAll over a subtree spanning BOTH stores: assert ALL members close (the cleanup-incompleteness bug — a single-store CloseAll silently leaves the other class open). Build a molecule whose root is graph but with a tracked work member.
5. drain cross-class: a synthetic drain convoy (graph) tracking WORK backlog members — assert convoy.Members returns the work members (graph DepList + work Get), TrackItem records the graph->work edge, reserveDrainMembers writes to the WORK member, ensureDrainUnitConvoy creates in GRAPH.
6. graph-only List parity (G2c): assert a graph molecule's scope-check / orphan-release / keep-alive member set via GraphOnlyListFor(policyStore) is IDENTICAL before (Router) and after (forwarder) — and that GraphOnlyListFor returns ok==true on the post-G3 wrapped graph store (guards the dead-again regression).
7. DIFFERENTIAL: for the same operation sequence, assert the post-G3 store wiring produces byte-identical bead state to the pre-G3 Router wiring (run both, diff the two physical stores). This is the create-by-class/by-id/claim coverage that router_*_test.go currently holds and must be re-homed before deletion.
All of (1)-(7) must ALSO be run at graph=bd and assert identical-to-today (the byte-identical default-city invariant).
contradictions: The trace:molecule tracer concludes the ENTIRE molecule package is no-needs-routing-store and that the 'faithful low-churn fix is to keep the single-beads.Store shape and hand molecule a routing store (graphRoutedStore) — exactly the Track-G design item 5'. This DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS the migration owner's binding rule ('do NOT silently build a routing-store object; the Path-B graphRoutedStore was rejected'). The tracer is treating the rejected design as the recommended path. My adjudication agrees the THREE specific sites (recipe-fallback Create, CloseSubtree/CloseAll, drain/Members) are by-id-irreducible, but this must be surfaced as an OWNER DECISION, not silently resolved by reviving Path-B as the tracer proposes.

The trace:molecule tracer marks SEVERAL ops 'yes-thread-graph-store' that are actually only conditionally graph (e.g. molecule.go:476 GraphApplyFor primary path) while simultaneously noting 'mixed/work pours reach the SAME code with a work store and must NOT be sent to graph' — i.e. the per-op verdict and the note contradict: if the same code services both classes, a fixed graph-store param is WRONG for the work pours. The honest verdict for the shared Instantiate entrypoint is class-mixed, which is why I rate molecule no-needs-routing-store overall despite many individually-graph ops.

The G2b anchor task title says return '[work, graph]' but the anchors:g2b tracer itself recommends [graph, work] (graph-first) and flags the [work,graph] literal order as a performance + mis-pin risk. The task title and the correct implementation disagree; resolved in favor of [graph, work] with a note that '[work,graph]' is a membership not an ordering requirement.

The anchors:g2c tracer's headline 'Option A ... No call-site code changes needed' understates its own load-bearing risk (buried in risks[0]): post-G3 a raw SQLiteStore does NOT implement GraphOnlyListStore, so the forwarder goes dead AGAIN unless G3 adds a single-store graph-only-list adapter. The summary frames G2c as self-contained; it is actually coupled to G3. Not a contradiction with code, but the summary contradicts the tracer's own risk analysis.

trace:dispatch asserts drain needs 'a small by-id class-dispatch helper ... NOT coordrouter.Router resurrected: a 2-element ordered probe with no Create/ApplyGraphPlan classification.' This is a distinction-without-much-difference relative to the owner's rule: a 2-element [graph,work] by-id probe object that backs convoy.Members/TrackItem IS a focused routing store (Path-B), which the owner rejected. The tracer's framing ('not a Router') risks letting Path-B in through the back door under a different name — flagged so the owner rules on it explicitly.
bottomLine: STOP and surface to the owner: sling and the graph-PURE dispatch handlers (retry/ralph/check/retry-eval/fanout/tally/scope-check/workflow-finalize) CAN be made class-aware with separate work/graph params (yes-with-churn, no object). But THREE sites cannot — molecule.Instantiate's recipe sequential-fallback Create (molecule.go:663, services graph+work+wisp through one line) + markFailed, molecule CloseSubtree/CloseAll over cross-class subtrees (cleanup.go:144), and dispatch drain via convoy.Members/TrackItem (membership.go:68/24 — graph DepList + work Get + cross-class edge in one call). These do per-id, class-mixed store access that is irreducible to a single param; making them param-aware = inlining coordclass.Classify + a 2-store probe = re-implementing the Router inside Layer-2/shared-primitive packages. This is exactly the 'caller genuinely cannot be made class-aware' case the owner said to STOP and report. Recommended resolution: a FOCUSED by-id-only [work,graph] routing store scoped to just these three sites (Option A, reframed and approved by the owner — not silently built), keeping coordrouter deletable everywhere else; fallback is Option B (keep coordrouter for graph only). Do NOT delete coordrouter until the owner rules on these three sites. Conformance must test PHYSICAL store residence at graph=sqlite against the legacy .gc/beads.sqlite location — byte-identity at graph=bd is blind to the orphan risk because there the two stores are one.
